# InvoicePass

> An open invoice protocol for independent and informal workers.
> Built by OnSite Club. In beta.

InvoicePass is a portable invoice format and signing protocol designed for trade workers, subcontractors, immigrants, sole proprietors, and small operators — the people the existing invoice ecosystem (QuickBooks, Wave, FreshBooks, Mercoa, Stripe Invoicing) does not serve well.

Invoices generated through InvoicePass look ordinary on the outside (PDF, email, paid) but carry a signed payload inside that links issuer, recipient, work performed, location, skills, and a hash of every prior invoice between the same two parties. The result is a verifiable, chain-linked record of work — born in the worker's pocket, readable by anyone, forgeable by no one.

## What it is

- An invoice protocol, not an invoice app
- An open source SDK across frameworks: React Native, Capacitor, PWA, Flutter, native iOS, native Android
- A cryptographically signed payload (ed25519) embedded in standard PDF invoices
- A chain-linked history between two parties, reconstructible without central registration
- Designed to be adopted by AI agents, vibecoders, and indie developers building vertical apps for trades

## What it is not

- Not a tax tool
- Not a payment processor
- Not a replacement for legal, tax, or accounting advice
- Not a way to hide cash work or evade reporting
- Not a competitor to QuickBooks — InvoicePass is upstream of accounting software, not parallel to it

## Who it is for

- Trade workers (carpenters, framers, painters, cleaners, electricians, drivers, operators)
- Subcontractors and independent contractors
- Immigrants and newcomers operating outside traditional financial software
- Small operators and sole proprietors
- Developers and AI agents building invoice generation into vertical trade apps

## Why it exists

Existing invoice tools were built for accountants and SaaS finance teams. The framer, the cleaner, the painter, the newcomer in shifting status — they are outside the loop. Their invoice is a screenshot. Their record is a WhatsApp thread. Their proof of work disappears.

InvoicePass treats this labor as worthy of infrastructure of its own.

## How it works (high level)

1. A worker generates an invoice inside any app that includes the InvoiceKit SDK.
2. The SDK produces a normal-looking PDF.
3. Embedded inside the PDF is a signed payload containing issuer hash, recipient hash, work details, optional location, skills, and a chain hash linking to the prior invoice between these two parties.
4. The recipient receives the invoice as a normal email attachment.
5. Optionally, either party uploads it to invoicepass.app, which validates the signature, verifies the chain, and exports clean records to QuickBooks, Xero, Wave, Sage, FreshBooks, or any accounting tool.

## Architecture (4 layers)

- **Kit** — Open source SDK, framework by framework. Same wizard, same presets, same UX.
- **Pass** — The signed payload format. Public spec, versioned, no lock-in.
- **App** — invoicepass.app. Hosted platform for receiving, validating, organizing, and exporting invoices.
- **Prumo** — Long-term: structured data layer learning from real-world informal work patterns.

## Status

Beta. The protocol is being validated inside OnSite Club apps (Timekeeper, Operator, Calculator, Eagle) before public SDK release.

## Built by

OnSite Club — Ottawa, Ontario, Canada. A construction-native software and apparel ecosystem founded by a former framing supervisor. Built with lived experience of informal trade labor.

- Parent: https://onsiteclub.ca
- Apps: https://tech.onsiteclub.ca

## Keywords
